item.title: I Profile Celebrities for a Living. Nothing Prepared Me for Tilly Norwood.
item.description: The A.I. actress on her craft, the future of film and how she definitely does not intend to murder us.

item.title: A University System Went All In on A.I. Now It’s Tearing Itself Apart.
item.description: California’s public universities spent $16.9 million on A.I. during a financial crisis, and the result has been chaos.

item.title: A Whole Generation Seems to Be Using ‘POV’ Wrong
item.description: Gen Z might be using the abbreviation for “point of view” incorrectly. But linguists think it’s exciting.

item.title: Can You Get Rich Quick Off A.I. Slop?
item.description: Brainrot videos are specifically designed to confuse us. They can also be a financial bonanza for their creators.

item.title: Could the A.I. Mattress Topper Beloved by Billionaires Help Me Get Some Sleep?
item.description: The luxury gadget promises to hack sleep — and relationships. But can anything?

item.title: My Family Member Is Being Pressured to Donate a Kidney. Should I Say Something?
item.description: The recipient would be his stepfather’s colleague, whom he barely knows.

item.title: Should You Outsource Your Morning Routine to a Chatbot?
item.description: A.I. wants to help you start the day. Its suggestions? Drink coffee and get dressed.

item.title: Can I Force My Spouse to Do Karaoke?
item.description: A ruling on a duet-related dispute.

item.title: What Steven Spielberg Taught Me About Fear, Catharsis, and Being Human
item.description: Hollywood is struggling, but Spielberg insists that the big screen is still the best place to work out our collective dreams, joys and sorrows.

item.title: Raphael Warnock Says the Supreme Court Has Done ‘Violence’ to Democracy
item.description: The Democrat from Georgia on what he sees as the moral issues of our time.

item.title: Scott Pelley on the Bari Weiss Era and His Last Days at ‘60 Minutes’
item.description: Lulu Garcia-Navarro sits down for an exclusive interview with the now-former CBS News correspondent.

item.title: The American Family Is at a Breaking Point. Our Politics Have Finally Noticed.
item.description: Society has treated parenting as a private endeavor. But what if raising children is also a public good?

item.title: Why It Cost This Man $250,000 to Help His Family Survive in Gaza
item.description: How Israeli trade restrictions and shadowy profiteers drove up the price of nearly everything — from food to evacuation.
